item.title: Marion Moreland Memorial Symposium
item.description: Marion Moreland Memorial Symposium — Friday, June 26, 2026, 1:00–6:00 PM — Larkin 200, Gerald Larkin Building (15 Devonshire Place) — A half-day philosophy conference focusing on W. E. B. Du Bois — 1:00 PM: Robert Gooding-Williams (Yale); 2:00 PM: William Paris (Toronto); 3:30 PM: Kwesi Thomas (Toronto/Simon Fraser); 4:30 PM: Chike Jeffers (Dalhousie)

item.title: Pride Toronto 2026 (45th anniversary, 'We Won't Stop')
item.description: 45th-anniversary edition. 8 stages, 300+ performers. Theme: We Won't Stop.

item.description: La plus belle saison with pianist François Richard.

item.description: JUNO-winning vocalist Laila Biali with two-night stand at Jazz Bistro.

item.title: Toronto Jazz Festival 2026 (39th edition)
item.description: 10-day 39th-edition festival. Cassandra Wilson 30th-anniversary New Moon Daughter; Hiromi's Sonicwonder; Ibrahim Maalouf T.O.M.A.; Norma Winstone; Laila Biali; Emilie-Claire Barlow.

item.title: The Next Chapter
item.description: Director Scott Wright will participate in a live panel discussion and audience Q&A alongside clinical, research, and community experts in psychedelic-assisted therapy in Canada. Documentary exploring healing, psilocybin-assisted therapy, and the courage to begin again. Doors 6:00 PM | Screening 7:00 PM | Panel 7:45 PM

item.title: The Algorithmic State: Sovereignty, Safety, and the Governance of Public Sector AI
item.description: Speakers: Dean Ball – Senior Fellow, Foundation for American Innovation; Shu Yang Lin – Head of Design, CrownShy; Jaxson Khan – Moderator; CEO of Aperture AI and Senior Fellow at the Munk School. The webinar examines how governments can effectively adopt frontier AI systems while maintaining democratic control and state capacity.

item.title: Book Launch: Paul P.
item.description: June 17 – Book Launch: Paul P. | Paul P. with Taylor Walsh (includes window installation opening) | 7 PM at TYPE Queen

item.title: Terry Tempest Williams: The Glorians
item.description: At Bram and Bluma Appel Salon, Toronto Reference Library, 789 Yonge Street.

item.title: The Algorithmic State: Sovereignty, Safety, and the Governance of Public Sector AI
item.description: At Online (Munk School).

item.title: Book Launch: The Shithole Opry Collector's Guide
item.description: June 15 – Book Launch: The Shithole Opry Collector's Guide | Derek McCormack with Kara Hamilton | 7 PM at Hot Pizza Studio, 1152 College St.

item.title: Caribana Official Launch 2026
item.description: Toronto Caribbean Carnival official 2026 kickoff with live performances. 2-8 PM.
